subject: [PATCH v2 0/3] arm64: cross-CPU NMI via SDEI

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson noted that the patch does not handle the case where SDEI is disabled, which would prevent the cross-CPU NMI from working as intended.

Reviewer Doug Anderson requested that additional comments explaining the mechanism be added to the code, so future developers understand how it works and can maintain it.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Thu, Jun 11, 2026 at 10:47 AM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

---

Ah, OK. This makes sense. I read the cover letter, but I guess this
part of it didn't stick in my mind. I wouldn't mind an explanation of
what's going on being included as a comment in the code. Then someone
down the line won't be left wondering.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new mechanism to deliver NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) across CPUs on arm64 architecture, using the SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure) protocol. The goal is to enable interrupting a CPU that has its interrupts locally masked, which is necessary for certain debug and observability features such as system backtraces and crash dumps. The existing mechanisms, GICv3 pseudo-NMI and FEAT_NMI, have limitations or are not widely supported. This series adds a new delivery backend using SDEI, which incurs no additional cost on the hot path and can reach a CPU spinning with DAIF masked. It does not introduce a new hard-lockup detector but provides a way to reach a stalled CPU for existing detection methods.
